title: Disney Park News — April 26, 2026
summary: TRON returns to Magic Kingdom after a two-week refurb, EPCOT festival queues climb, and Hollywood Studios hits a busy Saturday. Today's briefing in 3 minutes.

items.title: TRON Lightcycle / Run reopens after refurbishment
items.body: Magic Kingdom's TRON coaster is back operating this morning after a planned two-week maintenance window. Expect 60–90 minute waits all day as guests rush to ride it. Parkio Picks will surface walk-on windows the moment the wait dips — usually right before parade and fireworks slots.

items.title: EPCOT Flower & Garden Festival enters its busiest weekend
items.body: Saturday foot traffic is projected to peak at 10am when topiary tours begin. Test Track and Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind will hit 80+ minutes by mid-morning. The smart move: hit Soarin' or Remy first, then the festival gardens.

items.title: Animal Kingdom extends Pandora hours through July 4
items.body: Avatar Flight of Passage and Na'vi River Journey will run an extra 90 minutes after standard park close on weekends. Expect those late slots to fill fast — book around them.

items.title: Disneyland confirms a Halloween 2026 overlay date
items.body: Haunted Mansion Holiday returns September 6. Tiana's Bayou Adventure stays in standard mode this year. Refurbishment dates leading up to the overlay are not yet posted.

items.title: Hollywood Studios sets Slinky Dog Dash record wait of 145 minutes
items.body: Last weekend's Star Wars Day overflow pushed Slinky to a 145-minute peak — the longest single-ride wait posted at the park this year. Parkio's Skip For Now card surfaced it as 'plan around' status all afternoon.

items.title: Magic Kingdom Princess Fairytale Hall adds an early-morning meet window
items.body: Cinderella and a rotating princess will appear from 8:45–9:30am on weekends starting May 4. Watch the Happening Soon section for confirmed showtimes each morning.

items.title: Soarin' Around the World gets a soft refresh
items.body: EPCOT's flagship attraction quietly received minor projection updates last week. No downtime; subtle improvements to scene transitions and color grading.

items.title: Disney California Adventure's Pixar Pier night-time photo lighting tweaked
items.body: Subtle but noticeable: the pier now glows warmer after sunset. If you've been holding off on a sunset shot, this weekend is the moment.

items.title: How to use Parkio Picks when crowds are bad
items.body: On a 9/10 crowd day like today's projected EPCOT, Parkio Picks shifts strategy: headliners with sub-30-minute waits become rare, so the algorithm leans into walk-on hidden gems. Soarin' at 10 minutes outranks a 50-minute Frozen Ever After. Open EPCOT now and the top of Best Right Now is doing the math for you.
